mechanism_narrative: Elevated levels of the federal funds rate increase the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows and raise debt service costs, which reduces equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Over any 2-year rolling window, the level of FEDFUNDS fails to exhibit a statistically significant negative rank correlation (Rank IC > -0.03) with subsequent 20-day S&P 500 returns.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points over a three-month window signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening aggregate demand, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns as earnings expectations decline.
failure_condition: In triggered instances, positive 20-day S&P 500 returns occur more than 55% of the time, or the mean forward return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in industrial production and acceleration in consumer prices generate a stagflationary impulse that compresses corporate profit margins and equity valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The joint z-score interaction of INDPRO and CPIAUCSL fails to produce a negative Rank IC of at least -0.04 with 60-day forward returns over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During restrictive monetary cycles, month-over-month acceleration in consumer price inflation reinforces expectations of additional policy tightening, expanding the equity risk premium and generating negative short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: During identified monetary tightening cycles, above-median CPIAUCSL momentum coincides with positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of months.

mechanism_narrative: Growth in real retail sales signals strengthening consumer demand and upward revisions to corporate revenue forecasts, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns through earnings momentum and risk appetite expansion.
failure_condition: Positive momentum in RSAFS does not exhibit a statistically positive rank correlation (Rank IC < 0.03) with subsequent 20-day S&P 500 returns over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the industrial production index below its trailing three-month average signals a negative output gap and falling capacity utilization, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns as industrial earnings forecasts are cut.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 3-month trailing average
failure_condition: When INDPRO falls below its 3-month trailing average, positive 60-day S&P 500 returns occur in more than 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: The joint decline in nonfarm payrolls and rise in the unemployment rate confirms broad labor market deterioration, reducing household income growth and consumption forecasts, which generates negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term between PAYEMS and UNRATE fails to generate a negative Rank IC of at least -0.04 with 20-day forward returns over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below one million units and declining for two consecutive months signals a weakening residential construction sector and tightening mortgage credit conditions, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST < 1000 and declining for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: When HOUST is below 1000 and declines for 2 consecutive months, positive 5-day S&P 500 returns occur in more than 50% of triggered instances.
